name: Honda HRX217VKA 21" Gas Self-Propelled Lawn Mower

specs.Discharge: 4-in-1 — bag, mulch, side discharge, Versamow (simultaneous mulch + bag)

whatWorks: The Honda GCV200 engine is one of the most reliable small engines ever made — owners routinely report 15–20 years of service with basic annual maintenance (oil change, air filter, spark plug). In Canadian climates where mowers sit all winter, Honda's reliability advantage over competing engines is significant.

The NeXite composite deck cannot dent, rust, or corrode — a direct answer to the chronic rust problem on steel decks after Canadian winters. Owners with 10+ year old HRX217s consistently report the deck still looks new.

Versamow is a genuine differentiator: the twin-blade MicroCut system can simultaneously mulch AND bag in variable ratios, letting you fine-tune how much clipping goes back into the lawn. No other mower at this price does this.

Hydrostatic drive gives infinite speed control — not a few fixed settings but a true throttle. On uneven or sloped terrain, fine speed control makes a meaningful difference in comfort and cut quality.
worthKnowing: At $1,099 CAD, this is the most expensive mower in its class. The total cost of ownership over 15+ years is competitive, but the upfront hit is substantial.

Gasoline dependency means spring startup after winter storage requires a fresh fuel check. Ethanol-blended gas (E10) can gum up carburetors after a winter without fuel stabilizer — a real Canadian spring headache.
Annual oil changes are required and non-negotiable. The maintenance schedule is simple, but it's more than battery mowers ask for.
At ~97 lb it's heavier than any battery alternative — moving it in and out of storage, or up ramps into a garage, is more effort than lighter cordless options.

commonComplaints.title: Over $1000 is a lot to…
commonComplaints.body: Over $1000 is a lot to spend on a lawn mower. I understand it lasts forever but the upfront cost compared to a $400 battery mower is hard to justify.

commonComplaints.title: Forgot fuel stabilizer last fall and…
commonComplaints.body: Forgot fuel stabilizer last fall and spent two hours in May troubleshooting the carb. Runs great now but lesson learned — stabilizer every fall without fail.

commonComplaints.title: Heavier than my old mower, harder…
commonComplaints.body: Heavier than my old mower, harder to get in and out of the garage. Not a dealbreaker but I wasn't expecting 97 pounds.

commonComplaints.title: Oil change is simple but it's…
commonComplaints.body: Oil change is simple but it's still one more thing to remember each spring. My battery mower has zero maintenance tasks.

verdict: The Honda HRX217VKA is the closest thing to a lifetime lawn mower: the GCV200 engine outlasts any battery chemistry, the NeXite deck never rusts, and Versamow is a genuinely unique feature that no battery mower matches. The $1,099 CAD price is earned over a 15-20 year lifespan, but the upfront cost requires commitment. For gas-comfortable buyers who want a mower they'll never replace, this is the correct choice. For buyers ready to go fully battery-powered, the EGO LM2156SP is the alternative to consider.

realBuyers.quote: Bought it in 2009 and it still runs like new. Fifteen years and three oil changes.
NeXite deck looks exactly the same as the day I bought it, six Canadian winters later. Steel decks on my neighbours' mowers are all rusted out.
Honda engine starts on the first or second pull every single time, even after sitting all winter with stabilizer.
The Versamow feature is legitimately different. I run it at 50/50 mulch-bag ratio and my lawn has never looked better.

sources.title: Honda HRX217 Review — Lawn Mower Reviews

metaDescription: Honda HRX217VKA 21" gas self-propelled mower review for Canada: GCV200 engine, NeXite deck, Versamow simultaneous mulch-bag, hydrostatic drive. Honest on price and gasoline dependency. ~$1,099 CAD.
introText: The Honda HRX217VKA is a premium 21" gas self-propelled mower with the GCV200 engine, a rust-proof NeXite deck, and the unique Versamow system that mulches and bags simultaneously. Built to last 15–20 years with basic maintenance. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B084KG5Y9W) at around $1,099 CAD.
